data.narrative:
Oxford Nanopore Technologies is a public-stage biotech & health company that has raised over $1B to date. The peer universe comprises 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables: Tempus AI ($6.0B), Freenome ($2.6B), Recursion Pharmaceuticals ($1.8B), and Alto Neuroscience ($529M), alongside a broader public comp set of 3 tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers.

Peer-median valuation is the primary applicable method, as the target's revenue is not disclosed. The median across the 4 public biotech comps yields $2.2B, with the peer set spanning a range from $529M to $6.0B. A public P/S anchoring approach cannot be applied without disclosed revenue; the Biotech & Health sector trades at a 7.4x median price-to-sales across the 3 public comparables, but lacks the input needed for target-specific application.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.9B to $2.5B, centered on $2.2B. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set (11.3x spread between min and max) and the absence of revenue-based validation. The single-method foundation reflects limited applicability of traditional valuation approaches to pre-revenue or undisclosed biotech entities. Investors should weight recent financing dynamics and platform adoption trajectory as supplementary signals.
